_comment: WC2026 group Netherlands/Sweden/Japan/Tunisia, Matchday 2. Data provided by the user: MD1 Sweden 5-1 Tunisia; Japan 2-2 Netherlands. Sportsbook odds Tunisia 7.50 / Draw 4.33 / Japan 1.45. Neutral venue. Lambdas calibrated to align with the de-vigged market (Tunisia 12.7 / X 21.9 / Japan 65.4).

meta.venue: Estadio Monterrey, Guadalupe (Mexico) - Match No. 1000 in World Cup history
meta.stakes: Tunisia comes off a 1-5 with Sweden: battered and almost obliged to win to avoid elimination, debut of coach Renard. Japan drew 2-2 with the Netherlands (good display) but lose Kubo to injury; a win puts qualification on track.

home.name: Tunisia
notes: XI ADJUSTMENT: Renard's debut (motivator, won AFCON) + switch to a back four + must-win => somewhat more organized and purposeful. attack 0.71->0.76.
Fragile defense after the 1-5 but with Renard reorganizing: defense 1.26 (holds up).
Will go for the match: leaves spaces for the Japanese counter.

away.name: Japan
notes: XI ADJUSTMENT: Kubo OUT (knee injury). Creativity drops: attack 1.3->1.27. But keeps Nakamura and Kamada (both scored against the Netherlands) and pushes Doan forward in a 3-4-2-1.
The market already knew about Kubo's absence when setting the 1.45 odds, so the xG barely moves.
Solid defense but not elite: defense 0.9.

cards._referee: Istvan Kovacs (Romania), CONFIRMED by FIFA for match No. 1000. Strict referee (career 4.47; last 10 ~4.8; some sources 5.1-5.4) but moderates in elite international play (CL 24/25 2.71). Japan is very disciplined; Tunisia frustrated and physical.
